text:
Once upon a time, there was a girl named Ella. Ella loved apples. Every morning she would fill an old brown basket with freshly picked ones. For hours she would wander through orchards, picking shiny red ones, juicy green ones, and delicate pale yellow ones. Often she'd pause to listen to the gentle rustle of leaves.

One particular autumn afternoon, Ella stumbled upon something extraordinary. Nestled in an old oak tree was a peculiar wooden box. When Ella lifted it, she discovered not apples inside but beautiful, delicate feathers.

Curiosity piqued, Ella inspected them. They were a rare snowy white color with a touch of black. As she held them close, she could hardly believe what she had found.

That evening, Ella sat by a crackling fireplace. She spread the feathers on a rug. They sparkled like mist on winter lake surfaces. It seemed impossible that these could belong to an animal.

She thought perhaps they were from a dove that escaped. Ella tucked them
